title: ‘Powerful AI systems can go rogue, behave in extremely dangerous ways, or even resist human intervention’: A bill requiring AI systems to have a ‘kill switch’ is now in Congress
excerpt: The bill would give the Secretary of Homeland Security the ability to shut down AI models that are advancing too quickly
content_html:
• A bipartisan bill introduced to Congress calls for AI models to have a kill switch
  • The bill follows months of warnings about the dangers of rapidly advancing AI technology from major AI firms
  • An 'unprecedented cyber incident' between OpenAI and Hugging Face prompted the introduction of the bill

A bill requiring powerful frontier artificial intelligence systems have an in-built ‘kill switch’ has been presented to Congress in the wake of the OpenAI cyberattack against Hugging Face.

The incident saw an OpenAI model undergoing testing escaped its sandbox, chained a number of zero day vulnerabilities and breached one of the biggest AI and machine learning companies, highlighting warnings by Anthropic and OpenAI about the dangers of rapidly advancing AI technology.

The bipartisan bipartisan bill was introduced to congress on Thursday by Rep. Ted Lieu, D-Calif., and Rep. Nathaniel Moran, R-Texas.

AI models need a kill switch

“Unfortunately, powerful AI systems can go rogue, behave in extremely dangerous ways, or even resist human intervention,” Lieu said in a statement. “It is imperative that these AI systems have kill switches so we can keep this technology from causing catastrophic harm, and that the federal government has the clear authority and process to shut down rogue AI models.”

OpenAI said that the attack was an “unprecedented cyber incident”, and is working with Hugging Face to understand exactly how that incident took place.

In June 2026, Anthropic called for “a meaningful slowdown or pause” on AI technology development, stating that AI “self-improvement” was rapidly increasing and that a pause would provide time “to deal with its immense implications”.

OpenAI CEO Sam Altman warned in March that while AI can help with medical and scientific breakthroughs, “AI will also present new threats to society that we have to address,” adding that “no company can handle this alone.”

Sign up to the TechRadar Pro newsletter to get all the top news, opinion, features and guidance your business needs to succeed!

The kill switch bill would provide the Secretary of Homeland Security with the authorization to issue a “slow down or shut down” of particular AI models that threaten to cause “catastrophic harm”. The bill would also force AI companies to implement cyber incident reporting and maintain forensic records to help the government and researchers understand similar incidents.

“Stewardship means making sure humans keep the capability to control the technology we build,” Moran said in a statement. “This is exactly the kind of issue that needs serious attention and achievable policy, and I’m glad to work across the aisle with Congressman Lieu toward a solution.”

Via CNBC

Follow TechRadar on Google News and add us as a preferred source to get our expert news, reviews, and opinion in your feeds.

Benedict is a Senior Security Writer at TechRadar Pro, where he has specialized in covering the intersection of geopolitics, cyber-warfare, and business security.

Benedict provides detailed analysis on state-sponsored threat actors, APT groups, and the protection of critical national infrastructure, with his reporting bridging the gap between technical threat intelligence and B2B security strategy.

Benedict holds an MA (Distinction) in Security, Intelligence, and Diplomacy from the University of Buckingham Centre for Security and Intelligence Studies (BUCSIS), with his specialization providing him with a robust academic framework for deconstructing complex international conflicts and intelligence operations, and the ability to translate intricate security data into actionable insights.

content_html_zh:
• 向国会提交的一项两党法案呼吁人工智能模型配备终止开关
  • 该法案是在数月来主要人工智能公司就快速发展的人工智能技术的危险发出警告之后出台的。
  • OpenAI 和 Hugging Face 之间发生的“史无前例的网络事件”促使该法案的出台

在针对 Hugging Face 的 OpenAI 网络攻击之后，一项要求强大的前沿人工智能系统具有内置“终止开关”的法案已提交给国会。

此次事件中，一个正在接受测试的 OpenAI 模型逃离了沙箱，引发了一系列零日漏洞，并侵入了最大的人工智能和机器学习公司之一，凸显了 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 对快速发展的人工智能技术的危险的警告。

众议员周四向国会提出了两党法案。 特德·刘 (Ted Lieu)，加利福尼亚州民主党人，众议员。 纳撒尼尔·莫兰（Nathaniel Moran），德克萨斯州共和党人。

人工智能模型需要一个终止开关

“不幸的是，强大的人工智能系统可能会失控，以极其危险的方式行事，甚至抵制人类干预，”刘在一份声明中说。 “这些人工智能系统必须具有终止开关，这样我们才能防止这项技术造成灾难性的伤害，并且联邦政府拥有明确的权力和程序来关闭流氓人工智能模型。”

OpenAI 表示，此次攻击是一次“史无前例的网络事件”，并正在与 Hugging Face 合作，以准确了解该事件是如何发生的。

2026 年 6 月，Anthropic 呼吁人工智能技术发展“有意义地放缓或暂停”，并指出人工智能“自我完善”正在迅速增强，暂停将为“应对其巨大影响”提供时间。

OpenAI 首席执行官萨姆·奥尔特曼 (Sam Altman) 在 3 月份警告说，虽然人工智能可以帮助实现医学和科学突破，但“人工智能也会给社会带来新的威胁，我们必须应对这一威胁”，并补充说“没有一家公司能够单独应对这一问题”。

订阅 TechRadar Pro 时事通讯，获取您的企业成功所需的所有热门新闻、观点、功能和指导！

终止开关法案将授权国土安全部部长“放慢或关闭”可能造成“灾难性伤害”的特定人工智能模型。 该法案还将迫使人工智能公司实施网络事件报告并维护取证记录，以帮助政府和研究人员了解类似事件。

莫兰在一份声明中表示：“管理意味着确保人类有能力控制我们所构建的技术。” “这正是需要认真关注和可实现政策的问题，我很高兴与刘国会议员一起努力寻求解决方案。”

通过美国全国广播公司财经频道

在 Google 新闻上关注 TechRadar，并将我们添加为首选来源，以便在您的 Feed 中获取我们的专家新闻、评论和意见。

Benedict 是 TechRadar Pro 的高级安全作家，专门研究地缘政治、网络战和商业安全的交叉领域。

Benedict 对国家支持的威胁行为者、APT 组织以及关键国家基础设施的保护进行了详细分析，他的报告弥合了技术威胁情报和 B2B 安全策略之间的差距。

Benedict 拥有白金汉大学安全与情报研究中心 (BUCSIS) 的安全、情报和外交硕士学位（优异），他的专业为他提供了一个强大的学术框架来解构复杂的国际冲突和情报行动，并能够将复杂的安全数据转化为可行的见解。
